entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Extensibility: All You Need to Know
entry.content:
Hi all,

here is another interesting document I want to share: the new paper on Clean core extensibility for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, now available at SAP.com and in the PDF Digital Library. 

Best regards, Thomas

entry.title: Re: Join BSEG, BKPF, EKBE and EKPO
entry.content: yes i got a result, but it did not show me any views with more than one table. i only got e.g. a view where table EKPO was used, then another view where the table EKBE was used, etc.

entry.title: I have error message IDMX200 with F-92
entry.content:
Error while trying to use F-92  in MEXICO for an Asset

Can you please help? message " Unexpected Customizing error" IDMX200

Thanks

Alfredo

entry.title: Re: Join BSEG, BKPF, EKBE and EKPO

entry.title: Re: SAP PS Exam Guidance

entry.title: 2H2024 - Remove the disable option for the new training experience
entry.content:
Hello,

Does the setting of the switch for new learning experience remain unchanged (e.g. off if it is currently set to off) when the new learning experience is activated in the system with release 2H2024?

Thank you for answering the question.

Conny

entry.title: Re: Adopting General Display Name
entry.content:
Hi Harvey,

can we change the format of COMMOM_User_Display_Name for non-EC ? I thought it is something standard as firstname, middlename, lastname.

Thanks,

Meenakshi

entry.title: Re: Remote Client copy without Personal data (PA Tables)
entry.content:
Hi Siriam, what exactly details do you need?
I would use SAP_ALL as a copy profile, but I don't want to copy personal data (tables PA0000, PA0001, ... PA****). I would not exclude the tables, since there are so many PA-tables...

entry.title: Re: Can you remember the first time you heard of SAP
entry.content:
Hello,

For me the first day at job after completing CA seven years ago. I was part of internal audit team for a client who was using SAP Business One. This assignment gave me an opportunity to explore and learn SAP. Understanding and learning the SAP methodologies in accounting and inventory valuations was challenging for me initially but I managed to gain expertise with consistent learning and practice. Later, I joined in a SAP Consluting firm and worked on SAP B1 end-to-end implementations and financial reporting needs of the clients. I have cleared SAP Certification on SAP Business One V.10. I just started preparing for S/4 HANA Finance certification. Looking forward to great learnings!

entry.title: Re: SAP TechEd 2024 - Summary
entry.content:
@sonamthote 

Thank you for the summary.

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/fire-of-innovations-highlights-from-sap-teched-2024/ba-p/13892535

entry.title: Re: Error FWM 02151 Using SAP Business Objects 4.3. The t...
entry.content:
Hi Natalia, 

is it every 30 days? If so, this is OK. The OAuth Token from DSP expires every 30 Days. 

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/3407475

Cheers,

Martin

entry.title: Re: After simulation, try out validation. Identity Provisioning closes the loop with a fresh new tes
entry.content:
Thank you Abhi,

Run Now is the action/button next to the Validate Job. No typo here.

Best regards,

Ivelina

entry.title: Re: Migrating NodeJS CAP Application to HANA Cloud - hdbcds plugin Error persisting after disablin
entry.content: Hi If customer have multiple .hdbcds object , it's hard to move thousand of .hdbcds object to .hdbtable one by one. Is there any automatic tool can for that scenario ? Customer do not want to change those configuration code manually .

entry.title: Custom Field Type - Code list Accepting free text which is not maintained in Code Values
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,
Good day!

We have created a custom field with type as “Code List”. Currently, users can select from the predefined code values using F4 help, but the system also allows them to enter free text that is not part of these predefined values.

Now business wants  to restrict the entry of any free text in this custom field.
please let us know if anyone encountered and resolved a similar issue?

Thanks and Regards,
Ankit

entry.title: Re: Validate FI Document
entry.content:
Hi @Maheswar 

You might want to look at BTE 1030 or BTE 1050 and write your own rules for validation, since the posting is through MIRO

Thanks & Best Regards

Sanil Bhandari

entry.title: Re: We are looking for a Technology, Software, Gas, Oil, and Energy niche website.
entry.content:
As per SAP Community Engagement Rules, if you want someone to contact you, setup your user profile settings > preferences > privacy > show email address to.

Solution here: https://community.sap.com/t5/questions-about-sap-websites/how-to-post-a-message-asking-people-to-contact-me-by-email-address/qaq-p/13893518

entry.title: Objective Setting & Appraisals
entry.content:
Hi,

I’m currently working on configuring a new appraisal in SAP and was wondering if anyone has any documentation or books related to this process. I’d appreciate it if someone could share any resources or recommend a good guide.

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: Re: How to see message by using <xsl:message terminate="y...
entry.content:
Hi,

here a solution which is working fine in Integration Suite. The mapping is interrupted and the error message is visible in the log.

Thererfore you can use the error function available since XSLT 2.0.

Example: <xsl:value-of select="error(QName('http://www.w3.org/2005/xqt-errors','error:err0815'), 'This is a test exception!')"/>

You can find a descrition of this function here: https://www.w3.org/TR/xpath-functions-31/#func-error

If you use this functionality you can see the error message in monitoring:

Error Details
net.sf.saxon.trans.UncheckedXPathException: This is a test exception!

BR,
Thorsten

entry.title: New SAP Activate Implementation Roadmap for SAP Business Network for Supplier Portal Public Edition
entry.content:
In today's digital age, building strong networks is essential for both personal and professional growth. This principle applies not only to human connections but also to the systems we rely on every day—especially when collaborating with different teams, partners, and companies. Let's see how this applies in the supply chain world, particularly with the SAP Business Network product.

Previously known as SAP Ariba Network, the SAP Business Network is an innovative and intelligent product that connects buyers' and trading partners’ people, processes, and systems, forming a transparent, resilient, and sustainable supply chain. The platform offers a holistic view and promotes collaboration through shared data and workflows, applying network-wide intelligence to inform decisions. The visual demonstrates its functionalities to both the buyer and supplier. 

To make the implementation process more efficient and profitable, the SAP Activate Methodology for Business Network roadmaps are designed to deliver a simple, self-service, and seamless customer experience.

In line with this commitment, I am pleased to announce that our new SAP Activate Methodology for SAP Business Network for Supplier Portal Public Edition roadmap is now available in the SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer!

The SAP Business Network for Supplier Portal is a digital platform designed to enhance collaboration between buyers and suppliers. It allows an easy communication process, provides access to information and tools that streamline processes, and enhances overall efficiency.

The SAP Activate Methodology for SAP Business Network for Supplier Portal Public Edition provides step-by-step guidelines, best practices, and accelerators to ensure a successful implementation.  This roadmap is available in the SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer and SAP Cloud ALM, with content updates every two weeks. You can also see more on this SAP Activate Minute.

 Currently, the roadmap includes 113 tasks divided into 24 deliverables and 5 phases: Prepare, Explore, Realize, Deploy, and Run. These phases cover various topics, including training opportunities, access and configuration for test and production systems, trading partner connection, Q-Gates, Product Cutover, Go-Live, and enablement.

Each phase addresses different processes, progressing from training and configuration to technical details such as extensibility and integration. Below are the key components of each phase:

1. Prepare: The main goal is to prepare the customer for implementation by understanding the system through Fundamental training, providing access to necessary tools, planning the project, and allocating the correct resources.

2. Explore: this phase intends to refine the configuration set in previous tasks, plan, design, and configure extensions and security, and close the phase with a Q-Gate task.

3. Realize: the main activities are accessing the test system, configuring it, setting up the integration functionalities, and testing them. Furthermore, that is the moment where the connection with the Trading Partners will be established through selection, communication, and integration activities. The phase ends with the Cutover preparation, communication set up, and Scope item configuration.

4. Deploy: in this phase, you will review the previous configuration, execute the planner cutover, and go-live with the production system.

5. Run: the focus is continuous learning and improvement opportunities, continuous adoption, and reporting and monitoring activities.

In conclusion, the SAP Business Network for Supplier Portal Public Edition roadmap was created to speed up the product implementation and so transform the communication and collaboration between buyers and suppliers, enhancing productivity and efficiency across the supply chain.

Introduction of Blog Series:

In the coming weeks, we will be providing this community with more information about the new and updated implementation roadmaps for SAP Business Network. Our blog series will guide you through the project implementation approach, leveraging the SAP Activate Methodology for the following SAP Business Network products:

  • SAP Business Network for Supply Chain
  • SAP Business Network for Supplier Portal Private Edition (coming soon)
  • SAP Business Network for Commerce Automation (coming soon)

All blogs will be tagged with #sapactivate for easy tracking and will also be linked in this introduction blog for convenient access to the entire series. Stay tuned for further details in the coming weeks!

entry.title: Automating China VAT Invoice Process
entry.content:
China VAT Invoice Management, a product in SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, provides end-to-end management for incoming and outgoing VAT Invoices. In the app Manage Incoming VAT Invoices, which is part of the incoming VAT invoice management, you can manually bind related documents, such as supplier invoice and accounting document, to a VAT invoice. However, you may prefer an automatic solution that the related documents are created along with the VAT invoice and the binding relationship between them is established simultaneously. To fulfill this requirement, SAP delivers the BAdI ‘FICN_TXI_MGMT_PROC_AFT_SAVE’ (refer to implementation guide)to automatically create, post supplier invoices, and do the binding via OData APIs.

Information: Typically, OData calls are used to interact with external systems. However, in this case, the OData APIs and the BAdI are in the same SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. The reason OData API is applied, rather than other APIs such as Supplier Invoice BO Interface (BOI), is that the BOI is only available for cloud development, not for key user apps. Additionally, for cloud development, a three-system architecture (DEV, QAS, PRD) is required, which can be prohibitive for some customers.

In this blog post, we will show you how to automate the process when a VAT invoice is created by OData API or is uploaded to the Manage Incoming VAT Invoices app.

You need to follow the two-part procedure consists of the following:

  1. Configuring communication management (for OData API)
  2. Implementing the BAdI

Part 1: Communication Management

To consume the Supplier Invoice OData API, you need to create the following for communication configuration:

  • 1 communication user
  • 1 communication system
  • 1 communication scenario for outbound services
  • 3 communication arrangements

In addition to creating the above communication scenario for outbound services, you also need 2 communication scenarios for inbound services. You can use either of the following ways to enable the communication scenarios for inbound services:

  • Reuse the communication scenarios pre-delivered by SAP: ‘SAP_COM_0057’ and ‘SAP_COM_0546’.
  • Create the communication scenarios on your own.

Note that the required authorization for calling the supplier invoice OData is automatically assigned to the communication user. If you don’t want to reuse the communication scenarios for inbound services, you must manually maintain the authorization objects when creating the communication scenarios. You can find more information here about communication management here and below is a schematic system diagram in the case. 

Note: The screenshots below are taken from an SAP S/4HANA Cloud extensibility system x6c as examples.

1.1 Communication User

Log into the SAP Fiori launchpad in your SAP S/4HANA Cloud system. Open the app Maintain Communication User and create a user.

1.2 Communication System

Open the app Communication System, create a communication system with the following information:

  • System ID
  • System Name
  • Host Name
  • Port
  • Users for Inbound Communication, Users for Outbound Communication (the user created in step 1.1)

As the communication happens in the same SAP S/4HANA Cloud instance, you only need one communication system.

1.3 Communication Scenarios

Scenario ‘SAP_COM_0057’ is used for calling the Supplier Invoice OData APIs from the outside.

Scenario ‘SAP_COM_0546’ is used for calling the incoming VAT Invoice OData APIs from the outside.

Open the app Custom Communication Scenarios, you take the steps below create and publish a scenario for outbound services:

  1. Maintain the required data.
  2. Under the “Outbound Services” tab, add services and URL paths for both supplier invoice and VAT invoice OData APIs.
  3. Publish the scenario.

1.4 Communication Arrangements

Open the app ‘Communication Arrangements’, create communication arrangements with the following information:

  • Scenario
  • Arrangement Name
  • Communication System (created in 1.2)
  • User Name (created in 1.1)

You create the arrangements for inbound calls with Supplier Invoice and VAT Invoice OData services using the scenarios “SAP_COM_0057” and “SAP_COM_0546” separately. The communication system and the service defined in the scenario constitute a complete service URL. With the username and the authentication method, the service can be securely consumed.

You create the arrangement for outbound call based on the scenario created for outbound services. The outbound service channel is established along with the arrangement. With the channel, http client can be initialized to call the active outbound services in the arrangement.

Part 2: BAdI Implementation 

Open the app Custom Logic, create a BAdI implementation for the BAdI “FICN_TXI_MGMT_PROC_AFT_SAVE” using steps below: 

         2.1 Initialize the http client for calling supplier invoice OData service.

 DATA(lo_client) = cl_ble_http_client=>create(

communication_scenario      = '<YY1_Z_CALLSI_CHUGE>'

outbound_service                 = '<YY1_SUPPLIERINVOICESERVICE_REST'> ).

“ Here you use the scenario created for outbound service as the client is designed to call outbound service requests”.  

          2.2 Get the CSRF token.

 DATA(lv_request) = cl_ble_http_request=>create(

        )->set_method(

            if_ble_http_request=>co_method-get

        )->set_header_parameter(

            EXPORTING

                name = 'Content-Type'

                value = 'application/json; charset=utf-8'

        )->set_header_parameter(

            EXPORTING

                name = 'x-csrf-token'

                value = 'fetch' ).

DATA(response) = lo_client->send( lv_request ).

DATA(lv_token) = response->get_header_parameter( 'x-csrf-token' ).

2.3 Initialize the http client for calling VAT Invoice OData service.

DATA(lo_client_vat) = cl_ble_http_client=>create(

communication_scenario = '<YY1_Z_CALLSI_CHUGE>'

outbound_service            = '<YY1_CHINA_VAT_INVOICE_REST>' ).

2.4 Get the detailed China VAT invoice.

concatenate '/A_CN_TaxInputInvoice(guid''' <lv_invc_uuid>  ''')' into data(lv_rsc_ext).

DATA(lv_request_get) = cl_ble_http_request=>create(

              )->set_method( if_ble_http_request=>co_method-get

              )->set_resource_extension( lv_rsc_ext )->set_header_parameter(

              EXPORTING

                  name = 'Accept'

                         value = 'application/json' ).

data(lv_invc_response) = lo_client_vat->send( lv_request_get ).

              “ Note that <lv_invc_uuid> is derived from the BADI interface.

2.5 Prepare the payload for creating a supplier invoice.

DATA lv_payload type string.

concatenate '{ "CompanyCode": <lv_inv_cc>, "DocumentDate": "/Date(1722150000000)/", "PostingDate": "/Date(1722150000000)/", "DocumentCurrency": "CNY","InvoicingParty": "13100088",'

' "InvoiceGrossAmount": <lv_inv_amount>, "TaxDeterminationDate": "/Date(1722150000000)/","SupplierInvoiceIDByInvcgParty":"10000","to_SupplierInvoiceItemGLAcct":[{'

'"SupplierInvoiceItem":"1","GLAccount":"12610000","SupplierInvoiceItemAmount":"100", "DocumentCurrency":"CNY", "DebitCreditCode":"S","TaxCode":"J3","TaxBaseAmountInTransCrcy":"100" } ]} '

 into lv_payload.

 “ Note that the company code <lv_inv_cc> and the invoice gross amount <lv_inv_amount> are derived

 " from the China VAT Invoice OData response ‘lv_invc_response’.                                                   

2.6 Set resource extension and send the request.

DATA(lv_request_post) = cl_ble_http_request=>create(

              )->set_method( if_ble_http_request=>co_method-post

              )->set_body( lv_payload )->set_resource_extension( '/A_SupplierInvoice'

            )->set_header_parameter(

                EXPORTING

                    name = 'Content-Type'

                    value = 'application/json; charset=utf-8'

            )->set_header_parameter(

                EXPORTING

                    name = 'x-csrf-token'

                    value = lv_token ).

DATA(response_post) = lo_client->send( lv_request_post ).

" Note that you must maintain the resource extension here. If you add the entity type when creating the scenario, the http call will fail.

2.7 Bind the supplier invoice and the VAT invoice.

concatenate '<?xml version="1.0" encoding="iso-8859-1" ?> <asx:abap version = "1.0" xmlns:asx = "http://www.sap.com/abapxml">'

'<asx:values> <OBJECTKEY> <GJAHR>2024</GJAHR> <BELNR> <lv_si_nmbr> </BELNR> </OBJECTKEY> </asx:values> </asx:abap>' into lv_xml.

data(lo_add_ref) = cl_txi_input_invc_add_ref=>get_instance( ).

ls_ref_doc-cn_taxinvcbindgobjkeyformat = 'X'.

      ls_ref_doc-cn_taxinvcbindgsrcebusobjkey = lv_xml.

      ls_ref_doc-cn_taxinvcbindgsrcebusobjtype = 'MIRO'.

      APPEND ls_ref_doc TO lt_ref_doc.

      TRY.

      lv_success = lo_add_ref->add_ref_docs(

             iv_cn_taxinvccode = <lv_inv_code>

             iv_cn_taxinvcnmbr = <lv_inv_nmbr>

             it_invc_ref_doc   = lt_ref_doc

              ).

CATCH cx_txi_input_mgmt into data(error).

              data(lv_text) = error->get_text( ).

              cl_ble_trace_writer=>write_info_message( lv_text ).

        ENDTRY.

        “ Note that invoice code <lv_inv_code> and invoice number <lv_inv_nmbr> can be derived from the BAdI interface.

        " Supplier invoice number <lv_si_nmbr> can be derived from the supplier invoice OData response lv_request_post.

Feel free to leave a comment on this post below. We’re looking forward to your feedback.

entry.title: Re: Combining Data from Multiple APIs in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
As I suggest to most people, I would create your own custom table -- not use the built-in table component. Building your own table is pretty easy and gives you COMPLETE control on how it works. If you need help with this let me know.

Once you have your own table, handling the data becomes trivial. Get data 1 and data 2, then use a formula to combine the them into a page variable. Then connect the page variable to the "Repeat with" of your table row. And if you need help with this part, let me know.

In the future there may be templates that makes it even simpler. See this Devtoberfest session:

entry.title: Re: Retirement w/Revenue With Customer
entry.content: Hola, hay alguna sulucion a este error, me esta pasando lo mismo para Mexico

entry.title: Re: How can you model a Parent-Child Hierarchy with two K...
entry.content:
Hi Marco, 

I was struggling with Cost Element and Company Code. Did not find a solution. The Hierarchy with Directory worked, however I was unable to assign two fields as leafs for the hierarchy self. Means DSP allows only one field to be assigned as a LEAF (lowest level of the hierarchy). Workaround was to concat the compounding key, what did not look nice ... User has to choose the Company Code first, to get the allowed hierarchies. 

Cheers,

Martin

entry.title: How to post a message asking people to contact me by email address?
entry.content: If I ask a question where I want people to answer directly to my email address, how can I do it, because the Article 4 Unacceptable content of the Rules of Engagement forbids "Personally identifying, confidential, or private information (e.g., e-mail, phone number, ...)"

entry.title: Validating and Testing Business Rules in SAP FSM
entry.content:
Business rules in SAP Field Service Management (FSM) are needed for customizing processes. Whether it's enforcing service level agreements, approving time efforts, or validating data, business rules enhance decision-making efficiency. However, ensuring that these rules function correctly is necessary, which is where validation and testing come into play.

In this blog, we'll explore the good practices and methods for validating and testing business rules in SAP FSM.

Step 1: Why Validate Business Rules?

Validation is essential because it ensures that business rules perform as intended under various conditions. A well-validated rule:

- Prevents incorrect or unwanted actions.
- Helps optimize performance by preventing excessive database queries or rule executions.

Step 2: Validating Business Rules in SAP FSM

SAP FSM offers a validation feature within the Business Rule Editor that lets you test your rule’s logic before deploying it in production.

2.1 Steps to Validate a Business Rule

1. Create or Edit a Business Rule:
   - Navigate to Administration > Company > Business Rules.
   - Select a rule you want to validate or create a new rule.

2. Set Conditions and Actions:
   - Ensure your rule contains the necessary conditions and actions.
   - For instance, if the rule is triggered by a service call, ensure the condition checks if `${serviceCall.subject} != null`.

3. Validate Rule Logic:
   - Click the Validate button in the rule editor.
   - SAP FSM simulates the rule execution and allows you to review the input and output data.
  
   During validation, you'll see a simulation of the rule's effect on the object (such as ServiceCall, Activity, or TimeEffort) along with any variables or SQL queries involved.
Example: SELECT sum(datediff(minute, timeEffort.startDateTime, timeEffort.endDateTime))
   FROM TimeEffort
   WHERE timeEffort.objectId = ${new.object.objectId}
This helps verify whether the fetched data (e.g., total time effort) is calculated correctly against expected values.

Step 3: Testing Business Rules in a Sandbox

Once validated, testing business rules in a non-production environment is the next essential step before moving to live operations. This ensures that no unforeseen conditions trigger the rule unexpectedly in production.

3.1 Testing in SAP FSM Sandbox

1. Deploy to a Test Environment:
   - Before pushing the rule to production, deploy it in a sandbox or test environment.
   - This environment should mimic your production setup but allow for safe testing.

2. Simulate Real-World Scenarios:
   - Create test objects that simulate real-world business cases.
   - For example, simulate a Service Call with varying levels of data to test how the rule behaves under different conditions.
  
   For rules that include SQL queries, ensure that various data conditions (e.g., missing or incomplete records) are tested.

3.2 Example: Testing a time approval rule

Consider a business rule that requires approval for time efforts exceeding a specific threshold. The query below calculates total time effort for an object:

SELECT sum(datediff(minute, timeEffort.startDateTime, timeEffort.endDateTime))
FROM TimeEffort
WHERE timeEffort.object.objectId = ${new.object.objectId}

Some potential cases are listed below:
- Test Case 1: Create a time effort for an object and log more minutes than the threshold. Ensure the rule triggers an approval action.
- Test Case 2: Log fewer minutes than the threshold and verify that the rule does not trigger.
- Test Case 3: Ensure that if no prior time efforts exist for the object, the query handles this scenario without errors.

Step 4: Moving to Production

Once a business rule passes all tests, it is ready for deployment into the production environment. Initially, monitor the rule’s execution in production to catch any potential issues that may have been missed during testing.

 

Conclusion 

Validating and testing business rules in SAP FSM is a key part of ensuring that rule-based systems operate as expected. By carefully simulating scenarios and testing in sandbox environments, you can deploy efficient business rules that optimize your field service processes.

 

Thank you and best regards,

Jithesh

entry.title: Re: How to post a message asking people to contact me by...
entry.content:
Solution 1, given by Craig here:

You can ask them to send you a private message though and exchange emails there.

Solution 2:

Go to your profile settings > Preferences > Privacy > Show email address to, and select All or Friends only:

Tell the people to answer to the email address which is visible in your user profile settings.

entry.title: Re: Check if a cell is "Open" in SAC Script with IF state...
entry.content: That's it... Thank you so much...

entry.title: Re: How to post a message asking people to contact me by...
entry.content: Correct the posting of things information such as a email address or telephone number is not allowed, more to protect you the user than anything else as the site is of course crawled by search engines. You can ask them to send you a private message though and exchange emails there.

entry.title: Re: What's Export Import Sighting Record in S4 Hana
entry.content:
Hello,
Thank you for your question. I checked with the experts: The apps are not related to asset accounting.
Can you please provide screenshots to shed some more light on this matter? Thanks!
Kind regards
Henrike

entry.title: Re: Grant users read-only access to the context of a sap...
entry.content: Silly me. I think if the API itself doesn't let you distinguish those 2 use cases, and the destination mechanism in the cockpit doesn't let you control access at the HTTP method level, then you are left with middleware (e.g., API Management or a CAP service). The only other way I can think of is if the original API had different URL paths between the GET and the POST but that is not the case. I had also thought of using the CSRF mechanism to block POST requests but I could not figure out how to get the flow to NOT handle CSRF but there may be a way.

entry.title: Exporting Tables from Datasphere to Hana Datalake File System using Replication Flow
entry.content:
Exporting Tables from DSP to HDLFS using Replication Flow

Introduction

The below is the process of exporting tables from SAP Datasphere (DSP) to SAP HANA Datalake Filesystem (HDLFS) in PARQUET file format using SAP Datasphere's Replication Flow (RF) feature. SAP DSP is a powerful platform designed to manage, model, and share data across enterprise systems. By integrating with SAP HDLFS, it enables organizations to store large volumes of structured and unstructured data efficiently for advanced analytics, archiving, or cost-effective data storage. The RF within SAP DSP simplifies data movement between different systems and environments by allowing seamless data replication.

Connection Creation

  • Access SAP Datasphere
    Log in to SAP Datasphere: Open your browser and navigate to the SAP Datasphere home page.
  • Navigate to Connections
    Go to the Connections tab: From the main dashboard, select the ‘Connections’ tab located on the left-hand side.

  • Create a New Connection
    Click on ‘Create’: In the Connections tab, click on the Add Connection button.

  • Choose Connection Type
    Choose SAP HANA Datalake: From the list of available connection types, select ‘SAP HANA Cloud, Data Lake Files’.

  • Configure Connection Properties
    Enter Connection Details: Fill in the required fields such as Host (Hostname of the SAP HDL instance) and Root Path (Path of the HDLFS where the table data needs to be stored) under Connection Details, Keystore File (.p12 format) and Keystore password under Credentials.

  • Add Connection Information and Save
    Type in Connection Details: Provide an appropriate Business Name for the connection. The Technical Name will be filled automatically upon filling Business Name and can be changed if required. Once the details are filled, Click on ‘Create Connections’ to finalize the connection setup. Note that the Business Name, Technical Name: DSP_TO_HDL is example. Replace them with the required names.

  • Test the Connection
    Click on ‘Validate Connection’: Upon creation of connection, it will visible in the 'Connections' tab. Ensure that the connection details are correct and click on the ‘Validate Connection’ button to verify the connection. The successful connection test gives a message 'Connection "DSP_TO_HDL" is valid'.

Replication Flow Creation

  • Access SAP Datasphere
    Log in to SAP Datasphere: Open your browser and navigate to the SAP Datasphere home page.
  • Navigate to Data Builder
    Go to the Data Builder tab: From the main dashboard, select the ‘Data Builder’ tab located on the left-hand side.

  • Create a New Replication Flow
    Click on ‘New Replication Flow’: In the Data Builder tab, click on the ‘New Replication Flow’ button.

  • Select Source Systems and Table
    Choose Source System: Click on 'Select Source Connection' and select ‘SAP Datasphere’ as the source system. Post this, click on 'Add Source Objects' to add the Repository Objects present in the SAP DSP. Select the required table and click on 'OK'.

Note that the Repository Object: LARGE_DATA is example. Select the required table.

  • Select Target Systems
    Choose Target System: Click on the 'Select Target Connection' and Select newly created ‘DSP_TO_HDL’ as the target system. Post this, click on 'Select Container' to select the path present in the SAP HDLFS. Select 'Root Path' (Home Button) or the required directory inside the Root Path and click on 'Select'.

  • Configure Settings
    Choose required settings: Click the 'Settings'. Here, the Load Type and Truncate can be selected. Load Type can be either Initial Only or Initial and Delta.
    • Initial Only: Load all selected data once
    • Initial and Delta: After the initial load, the system checks for source data changes (delta) at regular intervals and copies the changes to the target. The default value for the delta load interval is 60 minutes. You can change it in the side panel by entering an integer between 0 and 24 for hours and 0 and 59 for minutes, respectively. The maximum allowed value is 24 hours 0 minutes. If you enter 0 hours and 0 minutes, the system replicates any source changes immediately. This can be selected only if Delta Capture is turned on for a particular table.
    • Truncate: If Truncate is marked for a database table, when you start the replication run, the system deletes the table content, but leaves the table structure intact and fills it with the relevant data from the source. If not, the system inserts new data records after the existing data in the target. For data records that already exist in the target and have been changed in the source, the system updates the target records with the changed data from the source using the UPSERT mode.

  • Deploy Replication Flow
    Click on 'Deploy': Select the 'Deploy' button to save and deploy the Replication Flow. Provide an appropriate Business Name for the Flow. The Technical Name will be filled automatically upon filling Business Name and can be changed if required. Once the details are filled, click on ‘Save’ to finalize the Flow Deployment. Upon Successful Deployment, a message appears for the same and Deployed Status can be seen in the General section of Replication Flow Properties.

Note that the Business Name, Technical Name: LARGE_REP_4 is example. Replace them with the required names.

Executing Replication Flow

  • Access SAP Datasphere
    Log in to SAP Datasphere: Open your browser and navigate to the SAP Datasphere home page.
  • Navigate to Data Builder and access Replication Flow
    Go to the Data Builder tab and select Replication Flow: From the main dashboard, select the ‘Data Builder’ tab located on the left-hand side and click the newly created Replication Flow.

  • Run Replication Flow
    Click on 'Run': On the Replication Flow page, click the 'Run' button to start the execution of the replication flow. The status will be displayed in the Run Status section. To monitor the flow, click on 'Open in Flows Monitor' which navigates to Data Integration Monitor.

  • Replication Flow Completion
    Completion of Flow: Upon successful completion of the Replication Flow, a message 'DIRECT run of REPLICATION_FLOWS/RUN for LARGE_REP_4 is completed' will be displayed. The status can be seen both in the Run Status section of Replication Flow page as well the Data Integration Monitor.

  • Scheduling the Replication Flows
    Create Schedule: To frequently execute the Replication Flow, select the 'Schedule' button and click on 'Create Schedule' in the Data Integration Monitor. A pop-up window will appear consisting of Frequency and Time Range sections. Fill in the required details and click on 'Create'. The flow will then run according to the specified schedule.

PARQUET Files in HDL

Upon successful completion of the Replication Flow, the data from the table LARGE_DATA in SAP DSP will be transferred to SAP HDLFS in the specified path as PARQUET files by default. This can be verified by navigating to HDLFS using the Database Explorer.

The DSP replication flow will create a new folder within the Root Path of the HDLFS, named after the table. The replication flow creates multiple files (part-*.<extension>) during initial and delta loading. The number and size of these files depends on the source table size and structure as well as change frequency (during delta loading).

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite - IMAP and SMTP connection to Gmail with OAuth2 Authorization Code
entry.content:
Background

I was revisiting Practical SAP Cloud Integration by @yeeloon-khoo on email handling section. I am getting error when trying to connect to IMAP and SMTP of my own mailbox from Integration Suite channel by using 'Authentication: Plain User/Password' option. Both Outlook and Gmail are returning a similar error like below:

IMAP

javax.mail.AuthenticationFailedException: [AUTHENTICATIONFAILED] Invalid credentials (Failure)

SMTP

javax.mail.AuthenticationFailedException: 535 5.7.139 Authentication unsuccessful, basic authentication is disabled.

What happened

I did some search and found that in 2024, main email providers like Outlook, Gmail, Yahoo etc. are phasing out Basic Authentication method and enforcing OAuth for 3rd party connection. SAP Note states that SAP Cloud Integration Mail Adapter is supporting OAuth for Gmail. I believe similar case to Outlook & Yahoo too.

3512455 - OAuth Authentication supported in Mail Adapter for Google SMTP - SAP for Me

'... starting in autumn of 2024, users must use OAuth with third-party apps to access Gmail, Google Calendar, and Google Contacts. OAuth is a more secure access method, no longer use a password for access... '

With this, it is no longer possible to connect from SAP Integration Suite to these mail servers with just Username (email ID) and Password. While 'Plain User/Password' authentication method is not the most secured option, I believe this is still very widely use method throughout the years. This is especially true for POC or test scenarios as it provides the most simple and straightforward use case.

I try to search on how we could quickly get the email connection to SMTP and IMAP works again for SAP Integration Suite. However, there seems to be no straightforward dummy guide specifically for SAP Integration Suite. Useful references are scattered throughout different sites and forums.

I did quite some trial and error and manage to get it works on Gmail. I am sharing the steps and hopefully it could be helpful to the community. I didn't try out Outlook or Yahoo, but I believe the steps to get it work could be similar.

** You might be seeing Integration Suite, Cloud Integration, Cloud Platform Integration, IS, CI, CPI throughout this article. But we all know it is referring to the same thing. Welcome to the ever-changing SAP world!!

Step-by-step How To

Gmail Section

Before starting, we need to have a working Gmail ID. For this showcase, I am utilizing an old email ID which I do not use for day-to-day work. This email ID does not have MFA setup. Logon to the Gmail ID and we are good to start now.

1. Setup Gmail API. Go through Google Cloud Console.

2. Create a New Project. Give the project a name. In my case, I named it as cpi-email-api

3. Once created, select the project, and search for Gmail API

4. Enable the Gmail API

5. Once the service is enabled, we will see following site. Click on the top left menu if we are not seeing APIs & Services page

6. Go to Oauth consent screen, select External -> Create

7. Give an App name, in my case sap-cpi-trial. Also fill up the email field, in this case I am using the same email ID logon in this Google Cloud Console service. Save and continue

8. Add or remove scopes -> filter by Gmail API -> select all 14 scopes -> Update. Take note of the first scope name, we will utilize this in authentication config in Integration Suite later.

* We probably just need the first scope https://mail.google.com/, but I just selected all in this showcase.

9. All scopes related to Gmail API is now included. Save and continue.

10. Add Test user email ID. This email ID will be configured in authentication in SAP Integration Suite later. In my case, in this case I am using the same email ID logon in this Google Cloud Console service.

11. Overview screen is shown after OAuth consent setup completed.

12. Go to Credentials -> Create Credentials -> OAuth client ID

13. Application type -> Web application. Give it a name, in my case cpi-trial-gmail. Create.

14. OAuth client is created with Client ID and Client Secret. Note the Client ID and Client Secret, it will be use in Integration Suite authentication setup.

15. We are temporary done with Gmail setup now. We will move to Integration Suite setup.

 

Integration Suite Section

1. In Integration Suite, create Security Material.

2. Create OAuth2 Authorization Code.

3. Fill up details:

Name - IS-GMAIL -> can be any name

Provider - Generic

Authorization URL - https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth?access_type=offline&prompt=consent

Token Service URL - https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/token

Redirect URL - copy this URL for usage in next step

Client ID - from Gmail API Oauth Step 14 (Gmail Section)

Client Secret - from Gmail API Oauth Step 14 (Gmail Section)

Send As - Basic Authentication Header

Username - Gmail ID which configured as Test User at Step 10 (Gmail Section)

Scope - https://mail.google.com -> if scope is empty, the connection wouldn't work. We apply the first scope from Step 8 above (Gmail Section)

The Authorization URL and Token Service URL is retrieved from Google developers guide below:

https://developers.google.com/gmail/api/auth/web-server

Additional parameters ?access_type=offline&prompt=consent added to Authorization URL so that refresh_token would present, and the authentication could work.

4. After the object is created, we need to go to Google Cloud Console -> APIs & Services -> Credentials to maintain Authorized redirect URIs with the Redirect URL value generated from step above.

5. Back to Integration Suite, Authorize the OAuth2 object we just created.

Choose the correct Gmail ID configured for the Gmail API.

Continue, after acknowledging the warning.

Ensure the email ID shown is correct, Continue

Done and success.

Hit Refresh and the status is Deployed

6. Create Test iFlow - Read_Send_Email

Sender Channel Connection

Sender Channel Adapter - Mail (IMAP4)

Address - imap.gmail.com:993

Proxy Type – Internet

Protection – IMAPS

Authentication – OAuth2 Authorization Code

Credential Name - IS-GMAIL -> this is the object name we created under Security Material from step 3 above

Sender Channel Processing

Folder - /SendAPI -> I am creating a subfolder SendAPI in my Gmail to place the test email to be pick up

Other variables leave as default.

Receiver Channel Connection

Receiver Channel Adapter – Mail

Address - smtp.gmail.com:587

Proxy Type – Internet

Protection – STARTTLS Optional

Authentication – OAuth2 Authorization Code

Credential Name - IS-GMAIL -> this is the object name we created under Security Material from step 3 above

Receiver Channel Processing

From - the Gmail ID which configured as Test User at Step 10 (Gmail Section)

To - any email ID which receiving the email. In this case I am using the same email ID.

Subject - any subject is fine for testing purpose. In this case Test Gmail OAuth

Other variables leave as default.

7. Deploy the iFlow.

 

Testing Section

1. Logon to Gmail ID which is configured as Test User at Step 10 (Gmail Section). Create Labels -> SendAPI. This is subfolder /SendAPI we configured at sender channel. Place a test email into this subfolder.

2. With default setting, the sender mail channel poll every 10 seconds. The email will be picked up and sent.

3. In Integration Suite Monitoring, we can see the message processing of the email.

 

Conclusion

It is indeed a lot more steps required in setting up OAuth for Gmail connection. I hope this article could be helpful to anyone who has similar scenario. Thanks for reading.

entry.title: Re: Review Booklet Designer Add More Analytical Queries
entry.content:
Hello,
I'm closing this question as the attempt to clarify the question wasn't successful.
Should the issue persist, please raise another question that includes the error message in writing or in an attached image.
Thanks for your understanding.
Kind regards,
Henrike

entry.title: Re: Logon cookie check failed:repeat logon error when logging in fiori launchpad.
entry.content:
t-code :sicf

service execute >click default_host>sap>bc>ui2>click flp>

and Click Change Button Icon then Click Error Pages

  click configuration tab

select define service-specific setting(if not selected by default)>action during logon >select deactivate login xsrf protection.

then save .

Henrike

entry.title: Re: Error when generating the program /MERP/CORE_CPMS_APP...
entry.content:
Hello Samuel,

we have also a problem with the AppCreator since 4-5 month, the connection is working for us, but in the Application in Mobile Services several information (Mobile Connection,...) are missing. Currently we were not able to solve the issue and have an open SAP Ticket.

But in the meantime we got several notes, maybe they are also interesting for you. With one Note, we got better a better overview for the errors:

3432545 - Aktualisierung der Mobile-Services-Admin-Klasse - SAP for Me

3472370 - SAP Service and Asset Manager Mobile Services App Create, Update and Offline Config Updates - Korrektur für GUI-Status - SAP for Me

3461252 - SAP Service and Asset Manager Mobile Services App Create, Update and Offline Config Updates - Parameterkorrektur - SAP for Me

3407687 - Lizenzartparameter zu MS-App-Admin-Klasse hinzufügen - SAP for Me

3407690 - Lizenzartparameter zu MS-App "Report anlegen" hinzufügen - SAP for Me

3432546 - SAP Service and Asset Manager Mobile Services App Create, Update and Offline Config Updates - SAP for Me

Regards

Björn

entry.title: Re: SAP Build App data connection with Odata Integration.
entry.content: Thanks for the update. It is always helpful to the rest of the community to learn from your experience. If you can share what the issue was and how you solved it, that would be great.

entry.title: Re: SAC Currency Conversion - Input Control
entry.content: I assume the warning is due missing filter on version. Please check if for each measure used in input control there is version specified or the version should be specified either in widget or page/story.

entry.title: Re: SAP Cloud ALM API & Integration: Several extensions of SAP Cloud ALM with SAP BTP (Part 3): Use
entry.content:
Hello,

I’m currently exploring the API capabilities and wanted to know if it can be called in the user context. Is there support for executing API requests directly with user-specific permissions, or are there any workarounds available to achieve this?

Any insights or guidance would be greatly appreciated. Thanks in advance!

Regards,

Thomas

entry.title: Re: Adopting General Display Name
entry.content:
Hi @antoniobkw ,

Yes, if General Display Name is enabled, it is not only used in people profile but also used in people search and other modules. For the scope of general display name you can refer to https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_PLATFORM/534356acc0ab4b0e8977ebfb2eb432f7/9e32eb6b21424b69ae6314f87fd53815.html

Thanks

Harvey

entry.title: Re: Error Response to Sender
entry.content:
Hi,

using this answer: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/how-to-send-http-status-code-using-cloud-platform-integration/qaq-p/12463057

You can set any HTTP response code in your error handling. Just set the header CamelHttpResponseCode with 200, 400 etc.

Best regards
Matti

entry.title: Re: Unveiling today: SAP Build meets ABAP Cloud!
entry.content:
Very detailed and informative blog, @michael_ameling 👏🏻
@PierreCol - thank you for the playlist suggestions.

entry.title: Re: SAC Currency Conversion - Input Control
entry.content: Dear William, thank you for your reply. I cannot specify one specfic version filter in the widget as I am using measures with two different versions (comparing ACT vs BUD). Now I have created a new calculated measure in the model which is using a currency variable. this measure is the base for my calculated measures in the story. once i switch the currency all currencies in the story get changed. is this the best approach?

entry.title: Re: Convert Binary file(not readable) to Base 64 format i...
entry.content:
Hi ,Maybe you can try this 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import java.util.Base64

def Message processData(Message message) {
    // Get the binary file content from the message body
    byte[] binaryContent = message.getBody(byte[].class)

    // Convert the binary content to Base64
    String base64Content = Base64.getEncoder().encodeToString(binaryContent)

    // Set the Base64 encoded content as the message body
    message.setBody(base64Content)

    // Set the file name in the message headers
    def headers = message.getHeaders()
    headers.put("FileName", "your_file_name_here.pdf") // Replace with the actual file name

    return message
}

entry.title: Migrating from Subscription-Based Data Model to Provider Contracts Data Model
entry.content:
Hello Experts

We are currently working on migrating from a subscription-based data model to a provider contract data model in our project. I would like to ask if there are any detailed documents or resources that we can refer to for guidance during this migration.

Additionally, could you please provide insights into the advantages of using the provider contract data model over the subscription-based model in terms of functionality, performance, and maintenance?

Looking forward to your inputs.

entry.title: Re: Attainment for Asset Management for Resource Scheduling S/4 HANA
entry.content:
Hi Julia,

Without further details, it is hard to pinpoint the exact cause.

Do you see the operations' status as completed in the schedule?

Maybe it makes sense to check the following SAP Help documentation links:

  • Key Information for Tracking Schedule Execution: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/8308e6d301d54584a33cd04a9861bc52/9a3262c243ef4c649eaeed4e63bf510f.html?locale=en-US
  • Schedule Attainment: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/8308e6d301d54584a33cd04a9861bc52/0887408d06964f17bd2ee68a44e0b3da.html?locale=en-US
  • When is an order operation completed: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/8308e6d301d54584a33cd04a9861bc52/79c52c17a7c147b18035d8c4679a5bd6.html?locale=en-US

Best regards, Markus

entry.title: SAP fiori elements list report application adding more than one custom filter will not working
entry.content:
SAP fiori elements list report application adding more than one custom filter

1.using the same entity set with different property name it will override the first one

2.using the different entity set different property name it not been displayed 

in both cases when add the controller code using guided development, error will be displayed like

The existing controller file contents doesn't match the supported template. Copy and replace the function manually. how to resolve the error

entry.title: Data mismatch in message mapping in SAP PI
entry.content:
Hello experts

So I have an issue here. I have xml data with structure like that includes :

<email_ids>
<id>12@test.com</id>
</email_ids
<email_ids>
<id>34@test.com</id>
</email_ids>

The occurrence of email_ids is (0,unbounded) so it should be able to occur several times without issue. The mapping is straight as source and target structures are the same. I just use map with default so the blank fields can be populated. Now, the issue is while testing the id field is populated ok once, but all the subsequent times, it either has a mismatch or it is not populated at all. Could someone please tell me why this is happening and how to fix this. 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Sub-processes Linking in solution processes
entry.content: Hello Sandro, Unfortunately maintaining subprocess target diagram is not available today in SAP Cloud ALM. We have a similar request in the influence tool can you please vote for it: https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/329099

entry.title: Re: Can the bank check configuration be changed to the sa...
entry.content: Hello @HS9 , looks like your question was answered. Please confirm or re-open if you have any other question. Thanks!

entry.title: How to generate a SAML assertion by Using SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS)
entry.content:
Hi,

Could anyone please share details on how to generate a SAML assertion response through API for SAC (SAP Analytic Cloud Embedded Lite) by using SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS)

 

We are trying to figure out how IAS could provide a SAML assertion response by API so that our application could call it for live connection at SAC.

I know that there is a tiger demo application which used to provide the SAML Assertion for SAC, however, our security expert suggest us to  call IAS API to generate the SAML response directly. 

I would need to know the detailed steps to generate SAML assertions by using IDP (SAP IAS) API.

Thank you,

Anna

entry.title: Re: Schedule publications SAC Stories in Excel file type
entry.content:
Hi,

I am creating publication on the Optimized story, but not able to see the Choose Mode option or Excel format. What could be the reason?

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: How to generate a SAML assertion by Using SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS)
entry.content: Below is the SAML Assertion SAMPLE we needed: <?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?> <saml2p:Response xmlns:saml2p="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:protocol" Destination="https://onntvudbzxtzccg8gxrtf8x.authentication.us31.hana.ondemand.com/saml/SSO/alias/onntvudbzxtzccg8gxrtf8x.canary-cf-us31" ID="_e94f9b08228c6b5a84b97c717ac1f270" IssueInstant="2024-09-30T07:51:59.185Z" Version="2.0"> <saml2:Issuer xmlns:saml2="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:assertion">tiger</saml2:Issuer> <saml2p:Status> <saml2p:StatusCode Value="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:status:Success"/> </saml2p:Status> <saml2:Assertion xmlns:saml2="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:assertion" ID="_5c8ef919fd221bb269ef69b20d86bd8d" IssueInstant="2024-09-30T07:51:59.185Z" Version="2.0"> <saml2:Issuer>tiger</saml2:Issuer> <ds:Signature xmlns:ds="http://www.w3.org/2000/09/xmldsig#"> <ds:SignedInfo> <ds:CanonicalizationMethod Algorithm="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/xml-exc-c14n#"/> <ds:SignatureMethod Algorithm="http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmldsig-more#rsa-sha256"/> <ds:Reference URI="#_5c8ef919fd221bb269ef69b20d86bd8d"> <ds:Transforms> <ds:Transform Algorithm="http://www.w3.org/2000/09/xmldsig#enveloped-signature"/> <ds:Transform Algorithm="http://www.w3.org/2001/10/xml-exc-c14n#"/> </ds:Transforms> <ds:DigestMethod Algorithm="http://www.w3.org/2001/04/xmlenc#sha256"/> <ds:DigestValue>DSxHzU0f7Ckvg8D48L7AJsiwLHJS6p2IiG1BErn2G3w=</ds:DigestValue> </ds:Reference> </ds:SignedInfo> <ds:SignatureValue> T86G0LCBtplHQQU61ylqV3m6XvECr3YF847+f9U30RtLR5tmJUbngvsqu91vXs+8CXBrW+HOOpqx Uy6VkU5Xmv5RRORK/rAKtWJIlyAXMUbsRNQ6qs83ynHIuQvwG7Yk7nmYORcy3ALGK10HS/hQ8e8P 9TIRlu5jLk7QhEpvkXowMRqhWCDc9XcOYtRJlcwxFa9Yd0jtj7YUsQg0+Ad4yPVwLpMkEfKHSoKc zrGd+mOgBrycyEQEQ0AMeuUyDIIoZG0MBxaLu/EGjUQGkaHVZ9ok6O/0vw37lfcqR5skupPX8qvm BWZYYnyHLZiff2+BIjS/RmKvGNfQ/FhQ4vCntw== </ds:SignatureValue> </ds:Signature> <saml2:Subject> <saml2:NameID Format="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:1.1:nameid-format:unspecified">jin.chen06@gmail.com</saml2:NameID> <saml2:SubjectConfirmation Method="urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:cm:bearer"> <saml2:SubjectConfirmationData NotOnOrAfter="2024-10-01T07:51:59.185Z" Recipient="https://onntvudbzxtzccg8gxrtf8x.authentication.us31.hana.ondemand.com/saml/SSO/alias/onntvudbzxtzccg8gxrtf8x.canary-cf-us31"/> </saml2:SubjectConfirmation> </saml2:Subject> <saml2:Conditions NotBefore="2024-09-28T07:51:59.185Z" NotOnOrAfter="2024-10-01T07:51:59.185Z"> <saml2:AudienceRestriction> <saml2:Audience>https://onntvudbzxtzccg8gxrtf8x.authentication.us31.hana.ondemand.com</saml2:Audience> </saml2:AudienceRestriction> </saml2:Conditions> <saml2:AuthnStatement AuthnInstant="2024-09-30T07:51:59.186Z" SessionNotOnOrAfter="2024-10-01T07:51:59.186Z"> <saml2:AuthnContext> <saml2:AuthnContextClassRef>urn:oasis:names:tc:SAML:2.0:ac:classes:PreviousSession</saml2:AuthnContextClassRef> </saml2:AuthnContext> </saml2:AuthnStatement> </saml2:Assertion> </saml2p:Response>

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud EPIC Integration
entry.content: Hello @Jessica-Jia , looks like your question was answered. Please confirm or re-open if you have any other question. Thanks!

entry.title: SAP CAP CDS Distinct does not return distinct values
entry.content:
I am currently trying to implement a distinct entity projection based on one of my tables. The table in the data base is pretty simple and holds onto users in differnt sap onprem systems: 

entity SAPUsers: cuid {
 userId: String; 
userName: String; 
email: String;
}

In my CAP project I want to provide a search field so an enduser can search across all our SAP Landscape for one user. The user simply has to type in the search field the users username, id oder email and the SAP User will be shown in the field. 

Unfortunately across our Landscape, the username and email is not always the same (but the ID is always the same). So it can happen that one user has multiple entries in the table but the same user id. 

To make my search field a little bit more beautiful I would like to show the distinct user IDs based on the first entry that is found. 

Therefore I implemented a projection looking like the following

    entity DistinctUsers              as
        select from SAPUsers distinct{
            key userId,
            email,
            userName
        };

Since I did not find any documentation about the cds distinct keyword in the SAP CAP docu expect the following question: Question Distinct my assumption was that the distinct would be based on the id field, since this is the key for this projection. 

Unfortunately when I create the distinction like that, users that have different email or username will be shown multiple times. I also cannot leave out the email or username in the distinct entity since I need those in the search field. I also tried out to put the distinct next to the select and create a SQL like querry, but none of it worked. 

Therefore I am a little stuck on how to create a distinct entity based on the ID field but also have access to the email and userName.

entry.title: Re: How to add fetch multiple lines of record from a CDS...
entry.content: Hello @Sampath4 , looks like your question was answered. Please confirm or re-open if you have any other question. Thanks!

entry.title: Re: Automating SAP DSP Task Chains with CLI Wrapping: FastAPI Integration with SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @klorn_pallier ,

can you share which commands you are executing?

Regards

Christian

entry.title: Pending documents are not showing under "Approval Decision Report" issue
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

One of my client try to see the Approved documents in "Approval Decision report", But system throw "No Matching record found". But the same its showing under "Approval Status report". 

Why the Approved documents are not showing in "Approval Decision Report". Kindly give a perfect solution ASAP.

Enclosed Screen Shot FYR.

entry.title: Re: Custom Field Type - Code list Accepting free text whi...
entry.content:
Hi

Please check following blogs that provide the detail about creating Code List,

Key User Extensibility: Create a Custom Code List ... - SAP Community

If you still meet the same issue, I would like advise you to raise an incident to SAP product support team.

 

Kind Regards,

Lucy

entry.title: Re: Ho to create a Testuser for RAP App in BTP?
entry.content: Hi junwu! I didn't mention it but Employee has been maintained as well

Hello @anshuman_s22,

I am facing the same issue. 

Have you found a solution to this problem? 

Best regards, 
Benjamin

entry.title: Re: Lumira Support for BW/4 HANA 2021 > SPS03

I've come across the same question - why does the PAM only say SP03 for BW/4HANA 2021?

 

Did you succesfully upgrade to SP05 and did you encounter any issues with Lumira?

 

Kind regards,

Jonas

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Plan and Measuring Point cannot be mainta...
entry.content: Hello This is known issue in CE2408. It will be fixed as part of HF cycle 07. This will reach your systems by the 21st of October 2024

entry.title: Re: new story cannot be saved: "APP_UNAVAILABLE_SELECT_NEW"
entry.content: Thank you @anantbavadiya. You suggest as I do: recreate the lost job, but you say it should be done in a new or refreshed page, thanks for this additional information!

Thank You

entry.title: Re: Maintenance Plan and Measuring Point cannot be mainta...
entry.content: Thank you for your response

entry.title: How to show/ hide Indicator charts in SAC dashboard based on roles
entry.content:
Hi ,

We have a story which has a model with data access and privacy enabled and roles created. The table data is getting filtered based on the role as well as the charts data.

But we would like to show or hide the charts based on this role.

How can this be achieved on the SAC dashboard. 

Regards,

Natasha

entry.title: Re: Set Up Trust Between SAP Cloud Identity Services and SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry environment
entry.content: If the trust configuration was initiated from BTP for a certain application, then this application may determine that only a certain attribute is allowed as subject name id

entry.title: Re: Are you affected by SCIP database of ECHA? Think about SAP Product Compliance for Discrete Indus
entry.content:
If you're looking for a standard software solution that includes ongoing maintenance, opesus software for the EU SCIP Database is the ideal choice!

Seamlessly integrated with SAP Product Compliance and SAP Product and REACH Compliance, it automatically detects and sends new or updated compliance data directly to the EU SCIP database.

And if you're not currently using SAP Product Compliance or SAP Product and REACH Compliance, no problem—the solution can work independently of these.

entry.title: Re: Automating SAP DSP Task Chains with CLI Wrapping: FastAPI Integration with SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hi @christian_willi

It's:

entry.title: Re: outlook.aplication - chose sender (from)
entry.content: Thank you, this was incredibly helpful and it worked perfectly. What a relief!

entry.title: Workzone BPA task error "Failed to resolve dependencies of 'CrossApplicationNavigation.js'"
entry.content:
Dear Community,
since Monday I can’t see custom tasks in Workzone in some clients' sub-accounts.

The error I see in the browser console is:
Failed to resolve dependencies of 'sap/ushell/appRuntime/ui5/services/CrossApplicationNavigation.js'

Tasks are correctly viewable on use MyInbox of BPA.

Does anyone else have this error?

@Archana Could it be due to some update over the weekend?

Best Regards

entry.title: Re: IAS instance error
entry.content: It seems that you have only default IdP (= SAP ID service) as trusted IdP's. For this instance a default trust exists and no application specific config (with application plan) is possible.

entry.title: Re: IAS instance error
entry.content: To setup a specific trust you first have to configure trust to a custom IAS tenant and then you can configure application trust.

entry.title: Re: Remote Client copy without Personal data (PA Tables)
entry.content: I am afraid that the profile SAP_APPL will not exclude PA tables... PA tables have class delivery"A", and Application data includes class delivery "A" tables!

entry.title: Re: Error opening Guided Development in VSCode
entry.content:
Hello Rutul,

I tried to reproduce your issue with one of our test projects that also comprises several apps, but did not succeed. In my case (VS Code 1.94.0) no error occurred. I could open Guided Help for all projects. Also switched one of them to UI5 1.124 so that I get ux-specification 1.124.2 - no problem either.
Thus it seems to be a special problem of your project constellation. 
Could you please open a Service Now ticket, comprising your archived project?

entry.title: FIORI Lauchpad "FILE NOT FOUND" for specific Active Directory User
entry.content:
Hello,

i have started to be the SAP basis administrator for my company a couple of month ago and am facing an issue which is very strange and where i am completely stuck now.

A new active directory user has been created for a coworker as he switched departments. We are using different usernames and password for AD and SAP, so no SSO activated.

Logging into a windows system with this(!!) AD user (not SAP user) and browsing to the FLP with its FQDN(!!), you are prompted with the login screen. After entering valid credentials, you get a "FILE NOT FOUND" error for every(!!) SAP user. If you call the FLP URL using the IP address(!!), it works fine. If you log into the system with a different AD user, it works fine using FQDN or IP. We checked three different systems, everytime the same behaviour.

So, bottom line: FLP does not work when called by FQDN for this specific AD user.

I thought it might have something to do with our certificate we used for the FLP, as i use a certificated signed by our private CA in the SSL-Server PSE. However, it has the FQDN, hostname and IP address in as SAN. And why would that only be an issue for a specific AD user.

We increased the trace level of ICM, but didn't see any error which would explain this behaviour. We already checked firewall, there is nothing blocked (and our fw doesn't filter in context with AD users anyway). The AD user affected doesn't get any group policies different than other ad users who do not have this problem.

We also checked /IWFND/ERROR_LOG.

Using developer tools of the browser does not show any error except the SAP server responding "file not found".

I know this problem is too specific for anyone of you to have a answer, but are there any more logs we can activate or take a look into ? I mean, if SAP says it can not find a file, there must be a trace anyway to show what happened.

Any help appreciated.

Regards

   Andre

entry.title: Re: Ho to create a Testuser for RAP App in BTP?
entry.content: Thanks for you reply Kai! I had a look at those articles and did all necessary steps (Maintain Employee, Maintain Business User) and would expect to receive a welcome email (the email address has been checked and is valid). When I try to use the SAP ID Service > Forgot My Password, I also don't get an email. Does the email have to be assigned to a SAP Account / ID? Which is not the case. It is only for test purposes.

entry.title: Re: 500 connection Timed Out while updating Scheduling settings SAP TM
entry.content:
Time outs will not be recorded in st22. Its not a system dump. Please check if this helps

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_TRANSPORTATION_MANAGEMENT/54cf405c9d9e4c96bf091967ea29d6a7/7f3c715186311f66e10000000a441470.html

entry.title: How to restrict CDS view through PFCG role without field restrictions ?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts.

I'm implementing one CDS view that should be viewed by all users who have a specific PFCG role .

I tried to implement it through access controls [  grant select on
MY_CDS_VIEW
where aspect pfcg_auth ...]

but this way my authorization object has to have a field mapped to my view to filter data based on that .. but in my case all users with that role will have full access on the cds data . how to Implement that ?

entry.title: Re: How to reduce the size of ODQDATA?
entry.content:
Hello @Hoang_hung123 

The note 2502498 - Huge amount of data in ODQDATA table list 4 possible reasons for ODQDATA growth. There are also dependent notes. Have you verified all of them?

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: #LINEITEM_REFERENCE does not show childs
entry.content: Hi all, i have found a solution by myself. The answer was quit simple: I did not expose the composition views in my service

entry.title: Re: SAP IAS User store
entry.content: In general the recommendation is to establish the Identity Directory as central user store for SAP cloud applications. See SAP's CIO guide for Identity Lifecycle https://www.sap.com/documents/2018/05/38ce7d25-067d-0010-87a3-c30de2ffd8ff.html. However the SAP cloud applications vary in adopting the reference architecture and so one should always check the respective application docu.

entry.title: Re: SAP IAS User store
entry.content: Concerning best practices I recommend https://help.sap.com/docs/btp/btp-admin-guide/setting-up-identity-lifecycle
